text: [Music]
that moment right there
was the culmination of two years of work
trying to prove to myself that it is
possible to swing like spider-man
this video is sponsored by kiwiko g'day
i'm jt
so i wasn't kidding when i said that
swinging like spider-man has been one of
my main goals over the last two years
in 2019 i got an engineering internship
with hacksmith industries trying to make
it real we made a great video but the
six weeks i had there weren't enough to
get the result i was dreaming of so then
last year when i was preparing for my
university thesis project in
electromechanical engineering i proposed
the topic investigating the possibility
of spider-man swinging as a novel form
of locomotion which is just academic
speak for i want to be spider-man and
somehow they said yes just
don't do all those parts that aren't
safe
so then after graduating i took some of
those parts that weren't safe and made a
video about them which you can check out
in the description but in summary to
replicate spider-man's web i made a
device that i call a hook chain built
with alloy steel and the world's
strongest polymer it was inspired by
velcro to work by wrapping around
objects and catching onto its own rope
with tiny steel hooks now i know it
doesn't look like spider-man's web in
the movies and yeah ideally it should
stick to objects instead of wrapping
around them but really my number one
priority is to swing like spider-man and
no other method i've tried in the last
two years has worked as well as this one
has so here's my old system and here is
my new one the old one is a cool looking
wrist mounted web shooter with custom
safe grappling hooks a backpack full of
compressed air to shoot a harness to
carry a weight a microcontroller to
electrically manage the firing and the
disconnecting based on the hand gestures
from some spider-man gloves the new one
is a hooky rope
what can i say simplicity is king
but to be fair it is a little too simple
right now because i still need a way to
hold on to and let go of the rope and to
launch the web at a target so let me
show you my new solution
yeah it's a pipe
okay so my solution was what if you
could make one cylinder that did
everything you store the hook chain and
one end the launching mechanism in the
other and then you can just hang from
the whole thing like a handle so i could
just make a bunch of these and store
them on a belt or something once you've
finished swinging on one you grab the
next one fire it and let go the old one
and as long as they're small and cheap
enough there's no problem just leaving
him there i mean spider-man leaves his
webs all over the city anyway and no
one's managed to pin the littering
charges on him yet
for the power source of a launcher there
are two main options people use
compressed air is what i used in my
grappling gun but designing it for
one-handed operation would probably get
bulking expensive
the other option is combustion commonly
used in things like potato cannons these
launches work by igniting propane which
expands out and shoots your projectile i
tried that and it did work but i wanted
a bit more power so although people
usually use compressed air or propane i
thought why not use compressed propane
to show you how it works please excuse
me for a moment while i chop my
prototype in half
so basically it has a chamber and a
barrel and you can see that they're
welded together at this flange here i
can fill the chamber using this cut down
schrader valve that allows flow in but
not out
and the gas is kept in the chamber by an
airtight seal against this burst disc
which is just a plastic circle that i
cut out of one of my university folders
then i use a piezo sparker from a bbq
lighter to zap across this tiny spark
plug i made which ignites the fuel mix
and that explodes outward bursts the
disc and accelerates the hook chain out
of the barrel
i should mention making some of these
parts was really only possible because i
got myself this monstrous seven-year-old
lathe
it took days to fix up but it's been so
helpful so far i'm able to afford such a
big machine thanks to this video's
sponsor kiwiko who i'm really pleased to
be working with and i'm not just saying
that because it's what's in my script
actually i reached out to them because i
really like their mission to teach kids
problem solving and
steam skills so they can grow up and
change the world they do that with
monthly kits like this cool hydraulics
one i just built and they can ship to 40
countries across the world but
unfortunately not back in time which is
a shame because i really would have
loved this as a kid i still have this
science magazine i used to love reading
well over a decade ago and it's it's
great but the problem is i've always
been more of a hands-on kind of guy so i
would have used the heck out of those
crates and if you or your child feel the
same way you can get 50 off your first
monthly crate by going to kiwico.com
built irl in the description
going back to the design my big
challenge here was that to properly seal
the disc needs to be pressed against the
gasket with a force equivalent to about
15 kilograms
to achieve this i first tried making a
two-part launcher that threads together
at the disc but that was very bulky and
weak
so i made these polycarbonate cartridges
which not only hold the hook chains but
also help to transmit the force from the
front of the launcher back onto the disc
and that way i can keep the handle as
one strong piece
to apply this force at the front
initially i bolted a collar on every
time i loaded the web shooter but that
sucked so i knew i needed to think of
something more ergonomic and that was
when i had a light bulb moment no
literally check out this light bulb it
uses what's called a bayonet connection
where you press and twist and a spring
locks it into place i wanted to make
something similar for the web shooters
so to replicate these little arms i stud
welded some round stock onto the barrel
using the world's least correct method
of tig welding
then i needed to design a much stronger
version of this twisty bit since my
design was a complex shape i 3d printed
it and here's how it fits on
unfortunately it wasn't strong enough
not to worry though by switching to a
more isotropic material and simply
redesigning it another eight times i
eventually got it here's my concern
though this part is under 35 pounds of
force while the web shooter is
pressurized and although it's strong
enough to take it i'm worried that an
impact might push it over the edge
yeah
so it really should be made of metal
i mean i wonder if i could have this 3d
printed in steel
uh
or let's just not drop it then now with
that settled the last thing to do was
add the grip and sew on the straps and
we're done
now for the highlights this web shoot
has got a low profile fill valve a
custom spark plug all stainless welded
construction a 200 bar combustion
chamber a heavy duty nylon strap an
ergonomic abs grip a clicky button thing
polycarbonate reloadable cartridges and
a custom twist lock compression element
building this was dramatically more work
than i expected
and now i have to make seven more of
them

fueling
you see like in a car getting the air

so i built this fuel rig to fill one of
the launchers i rested here turn two
levers screw on the valve turn the other
levers unscrew and we're good to go
so
let's go find a suitable testing

i'm going to try swinging from now in

goes for it tally ho and it works fairly
